items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2014-9222
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2014-9222 surged from 12% to 87% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2014-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. Misfortune Cookie vulnerability in the RomPager embedded web server on residential gateway routers allows remote attackers to gain administrative access via a crafted cookie, affecting versions prior to 4.34.
items.description: Misfortune Cookie vulnerability in the RomPager embedded web server on residential gateway routers allows remote attackers to gain administrative access via a crafted cookie, affecting versions prior to 4.34.

priority.reason: EPSS 87% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 6.1. Priority floored at 'Patch within 48h': EPSS >= 70% is a strong exploitation signal even before KEV listing.

impact.how_to_check: Identify systems running the software class described in the report and review vendor advisories for applicability.

w5h.upgrade_action: Review the source advisory for remediation guidance
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because EPSS puts exploitation probability at 87% within 30 days; edge/network devices are internet-facing and a top initial-access vector.

items.title: Attackers exploit zero-day in Ivanti Connect Secure before patch availability

w5h.upgrade_action: Apply current vendor patches for Ivanti Connect Secure per the advisory
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because EPSS puts exploitation probability at 77% within 30 days; edge/network devices are internet-facing and a top initial-access vector.

impact.affected_versions: Ivanti Connect Secure (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Identify all internet-facing Ivanti Connect Secure devices. Check firmware versions against the advisory, and review access logs for unauthorized access, unusual processes, or configuration changes.

items.title: LockBit affiliates exploit critical SimpleHelp flaw to deploy Djinn Stealer

w5h.upgrade_action: Patch/upgrade affected systems — affected: Versions prior to 5.5.11
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because EPSS puts exploitation probability at 94% within 30 days; critical severity means full compromise is likely if reached.

impact.how_to_check: Identify systems running the software class described in the report and review vendor advisories for applicability.

items.title: SimpleHelp Remote Access Software Vulnerability
items.description: SimpleHelp remote access software contains a critical vulnerability actively exploited by LockBit affiliates to deploy stealer malware, enabling remote code execution on internet-facing servers.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). Known ransomware campaign use. EPSS 94% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 9.8.

w5h.upgrade_action: Apply current vendor patches for SimpleHelp per the advisory (CISA remediation due date: 2026-07-29)
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because exploitation is confirmed or actively occurring, not theoretical; EPSS puts exploitation probability at 94% within 30 days; critical severity means full compromise is likely if reached.

impact.affected_versions: SimpleHelp (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Inventory all SimpleHelp installations and compare versions against the advisory. Review logs on matching hosts for unusual authentication or administrative actions.
